a. The angel Gabriel announced John’s birth to his father Zacharias as he offered incense in the temple.
9. What sign did Gabriel give to Zacharias when he did not believe the word of the angel?
a. Zacharias could not speak until John was born.
10. What did Zacharias prophesy about John?
a. Zacharias said that John will be the prophet of the Highest to go before the Lord Jesus to prepare his ways.
Memory verse: “Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people, and hath raised up an horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David.”—Luke 1:68–69
Malachi; Luke 1:1–25, 57–80
Lesson 1: Malachi to John the Baptist
1. What did the angel Gabriel tell the virgin Mary?
a. Gabriel told Mary that she would have a son whose name is Jesus because he would save his people from their sins.
2. What else did Gabriel tell Mary?
a. Gabriel said that God would give Jesus the throne of his father David.
3. What did the angel of Jehovah tell Joseph when he found out that Mary was pregnant?
a. The angel told Joseph to take Mary as his wife because she was pregnant by the Holy Ghost.
4. Why did Joseph and Mary travel to Bethlehem?
a. Joseph and Mary went to Bethlehem because Caesar Augustus had made a decree that all the world should be taxed.
5. What happened when Joseph and Mary arrived in Bethlehem, the city of David?
a. There was no room for them to stay in the inn.
6. What sign accompanied the birth of Jesus?
a. Mary laid Jesus in a manger for his cradle.
7. What did the angel of Jehovah tell the shepherds who were keeping their flocks that night?
a. The angel announced that born that day was a savior who is Christ the Lord.
8. What did Joseph and Mary do to Jesus when he was eight days old?
a. They circumcised Jesus according to the law of Moses.
9. What did that circumcision mean?
a. Jesus’ circumcision meant that he was born under the law in order to free us from the curse of the law.
10. Who met Joseph and Mary in the temple when they took Jesus there?
a. Joseph and Mary were met by Simeon and Anna, who were very old and part of the elect remnant still looking for the savior to come.
Memory verse: “She shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.”—Matthew 1:21
Matthew 1–2; Luke 2
Lesson 2: The Birth of Jesus
1. Who came to worship Jesus in Bethlehem?
a. Wise men came from the East because they had seen Jesus’ star.
2. Where did the wise men go first to find Jesus?
a. The wise men went to Jerusalem to wicked King Herod, who told them to come back and tell him where to find the baby
3. Where did Herod’s scribes tell the wise men to look for Jesus?
a. The scribes said to look in Bethlehem because the Old Testament says that Jesus would be born there.
4. Who warned the wise men not to return to King Herod?
a. God warned the wise men in a dream.
5. What did Herod do when the wise men did not come back and tell him where Jesus was?
a. Herod killed all the babies two years old and under in Bethlehem.
6. How did Jesus escape that destruction?
a. The angel of Jehovah warned Joseph in a dream to flee to Egypt.
7. Where did Joseph and Mary take Jesus after God called them from Egypt?
a. Joseph and Mary went to live in Nazareth again so that Jesus could be called a Nazarene.
8. What happened when Jesus was twelve years old and his parents took him to the temple?
a. When Joseph and Mary went home, they did not know that Jesus stayed in the temple.
9. Where did Joseph and Mary find Jesus?
a. Joseph and Mary found Jesus in the temple among the scribes, asking and answering hard questions.
10. What was Jesus’s answer when his mother said that she and Joseph had been looking for him.
a. Jesus said to his mother, “I must be about my Father’s business.”
Memory verse: “When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.”— Hosea 11:1
Matthew 2; Luke 2:39–52
Lesson 3: Jesus’ Youth
1. What did John the Baptist wear and eat when he lived in the wilderness?
a. John the Baptist wore a coat of camel’s hair and a leather girdle, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
2. Why did John live in the wilderness?
a. John lived in the wilderness because it was a picture of the deadness of the people, so that their salvation was impossible apart from Christ.
3. What was John’s calling as the forerunner of Jesus?
a. John was called to prepare the way for Jesus.
4. What did John say concerning Jesus and baptism?
a. John said that he baptized with water, but Jesus would baptize with the Holy Ghost.
5. What did John preach in light of the coming of Jesus and his kingdom?
a. John told the people to repent because the kingdom of heaven was at hand.
6. What is the kingdom of heaven?
a. The kingdom of heaven is God’s gracious rule of his people in Christ.
7. How does God bring his people into his kingdom?
a. God forgives their sins for Christ’s sake.
8. How did John’s ministry come to an end?
a. Herod put John in prison because John had rebuked Herod for marrying his brother’s wife.
9. How did John die?
a. Herod’s wicked wife convinced her daughter Salome to ask for the head of John on a charger.
10. What did Jesus say about John the Baptist?
a. Jesus said that John was the greatest of the prophets.
Memory verse: “The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.”—Isaiah 40:3
Matthew 3; Matthew 14:1–12; Mark 1:1–11; Mark 6:14–29; Luke 1:80; Luke 3; John 1:6– 9, 19–37
Lesson 4: The Ministry of John the Baptist
1. What did Jesus tell John the Baptist to do?
a. Jesus told John to baptize him.
2. Of what is baptism a sign?
a. Baptism is a sign of the washing away of sins through the blood of Christ.
3. Why was the baptism of Jesus necessary?
a. Jesus had to be baptized as a sign that he bore the sins of his people and washed them away on the cross.
4. What did John see after Jesus was baptized?
a. John saw the Spirit of God descend from heaven on Jesus in the form of a dove.
5. Where did the Spirit drive Jesus after his baptism?
a. The Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness to be tempted of the devil.
6. What was the devil’s first temptation of Jesus?
a. The devil said to Jesus, “If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.”
7. What did Jesus say to the devil?
a. Jesus said, “Man shall not live by bread alone but by every word from the mouth of God.”
8. What was the devil’s second temptation?
a. The devil took Jesus to the pinnacle of the temple and said to him, “Cast thyself down.”
9. What did Jesus say to the devil?
a. Jesus said, “Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.”
10. What was the devil’s third temptation after he took Jesus to a high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory?
a. The devil said to Jesus, “All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.”
11. What did Jesus say to the devil?
a. Jesus said, “Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.”
Memory verse: “The Holy Ghost descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him, and a voice came from heaven, which said, Thou art my beloved Son; in thee I am well-pleased.”—Luke 3:22
Lesson 5: Jesus’ Baptism and Temptations
1. What miracle did Jesus perform at a wedding feast in Cana of Galilee?
a. Jesus turned water into wine.
2. What do we read of that miracle?
a. Turning water into wine was the beginning of the miracles that Jesus performed.
3. What is the meaning of the beginning of miracles?
a. It means that the first miracle was a sign of Jesus’ work to change the misery of the earthly into the joy of the heavenly.
4. How did Jesus begin to call his disciples?
a. Jesus found Peter and Andrew mending their nets and Jesus told them to go fishing.
5. What did Peter tell Jesus about fishing?
a. Peter said that the men had fished all night and caught nothing, but at Jesus’ word Peter would go fishing.
6. What happened when the fishermen cast their nets out into the sea?
a. The fishermen caught so many fish that their nets were ready to break.
7. Whom did Peter and Andrew call to help them bring in the nets.
a. Peter and Andrew called James and John to help.
8. What did Jesus tell Peter, Andrew, James, and John at that time?
a. Jesus told them to follow him, and he would make them fishers of men.
9. What did the miraculous catch of fish show?
a. The miraculous catch showed that the Son of God gathers his elect church by his Spirit and Word.
10. How many men did Jesus call as his disciples?
a. Jesus called twelve men to follow him and said that he would send them to preach the gospel.
Memory verse: “When it was day, he called unto him his disciples: and of them he chose twelve, whom also he named apostles.”—Luke 6:13
Lesson 6: Calling the Disciples and the Wedding Feast at Cana
1. Where did Jesus labor for many months?
a. Jesus labored in Galilee, where he preached the gospel of the kingdom, healed many sick, and cast out many devils.
2. Whom did Jesus meet in a synagogue in Capernaum?
a. Jesus met a man who had an unclean spirit.
3. What did that devil say to Jesus?
a. The devil said, “Art thou come to destroy us? I know that thou art the Holy One of God.”
4. What did Jesus tell the devil?
a. Jesus told the devil to be quiet and to come out of the man.
5. What did the devil do when Jesus commanded him to come out?
a. The devil threw the man down and came out of him.
6. What did the people say about Jesus after he had cast out that devil from the man in the synagogue of Capernaum?
a. The people said, “With authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they obey him.”
7. Whom did Jesus heal in Galilee?
a. Jesus healed a man full of leprosy who met Jesus and asked him to heal his leprosy.
8. What did Jesus do?
a. Jesus touched the man and said, “Be thou clean.”
9. What did Jesus tell the healed leper to do?
a. Jesus told the man to show himself to the priests as Moses had commanded.
10. Why did Jesus send the man to the priests?
a. Jesus sent the man as a testimony against the priests’ unbelief in Jesus as the fulfillment of the whole law.
Memory verse: “For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth”—Romans 10:4
Lesson 7: Jesus Casts out a Devil and Heals a Leper
1. What did Jesus do when he came to the synagogue of Nazareth?
a. Jesus read from the book of Isaiah about the one whom God had anointed with his Spirit.
2. What did Jesus tell the people of Nazareth?
a. Jesus said that the prophecy in Isaiah was fulfilled that day in himself.
3. What did Jesus tell the people when they rejected his word?
a. Jesus said that Elijah had gone to a widow of Sidon, and Elisha had healed Naaman a Syrian.
4. What effect did Jesus’ words have on the people of Nazareth?
a. The people were very angry, and they tried to push Jesus off a cliff, but Jesus passed through the midst of them.
5. What contrasted with the unbelief of the people of Nazareth?
a. A centurion sent the elders of Capernaum to ask Jesus to heal his servant.
6. How did the centurion show his faith?
a. The centurion said, “Lord I am not worthy for you to come under my roof, but speak only and my servant will be healed.”
7. What did Jesus say about that evidence of the centurion’s faith?
a. Jesus said he had not found so great faith in Israel.
8. What else did Jesus say at that time?
a. Jesus said that he would gather many from the Gentiles, who would sit down with Abraham in the kingdom of heaven, but many in Israel would perish.
9. What did Jesus tell the centurion?
a. Jesus said that as the centurion believed, his servant was healed.
10. What did that miracle show?
a. That miracle showed the power of Christ’s word to save his people by faith alone.

Memory verse: “After this I beheld, and, lo, a great multitude, which no man could number, of all nations, and kindreds, and people, and tongues, stood before the throne, and before the Lamb, clothed with white robes, and palms in their hands.”—Revelation 7:9
Isaiah 61:1–2; Matthew 8:5–13; Matthew 13:53–58; Mark 6:1–6; Luke 4:14–32; Luke 7:1–10
Lesson 8: Jesus in Nazareth and Healing the Centurion’s Servant
1. What do we read of the great multitudes that followed Jesus?
a. In the multitudes were many sick and devil possessed, and Jesus healed them all.
2. What did the many sick and devil possessed try to do to Jesus?
a. The sick and devil possessed tried to touch Jesus.
3. Why did they try to touch Jesus?
a. They tried to touch Jesus because virtue and power went out of him to heal them.
4. Of what was that touch a sign?
a. That touch was a sign of faith, which is union with Christ by which he forgives our sins and delivers us from the devil’s power.
5. Where did Jesus take his disciples to get away from the multitudes?
a. Jesus took a boat across the Sea of Galilee to the country of the Gadarenes.
6. What happened when Jesus and his disciples were on the Sea of Galilee?
a. Jesus fell asleep in the boat, and a great storm arose.
7. What did the frightened disciples do?
a. The disciples cried out, “Master, we perish!”
8. What did Jesus do?
a. Jesus rebuked the storm and the winds were quiet and the waves stopped.
9. Who was in that storm?
a. The devil was in the storm, trying to destroy Jesus and all his disciples.
10. What does the miracle of stilling the storm show?
a. That miracle shows that Jesus is sovereign over all things and that his church is safe with him.
Memory verse: “The men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him!”—Matthew 8:27
Lesson 9: Virtue Went Out of Jesus and Stilling the Storm
1. Who met Jesus out of the tombs when he came into the country of the Gadarenes?
a. Jesus was met by two men possessed with devils who roared like animals and cut themselves day and night.
2. What else does scripture say about those two men?
a. They were very fierce, and when others bound them, the men broke off the chains as if they were nothing.
3. What was inside those two men?
a. They were possessed by an unclean spirit.
4. What did the men do?
a. The men came and bowed before Jesus and asked Jesus not to torment them.
5. What did the unclean spirit say when Jesus asked his name?
a. The unclean spirit said, “My name is Legion: for we are many.”
6. What did the spirits ask Jesus when he cast them out?
a. The unclean spirits asked to go into a herd of about two thousand swine feeding nearby.
7. What happened when the unclean spirits entered the swine?
a. The swine ran down the hill into the sea and were all drowned.
8. Of whom were the swine a type, or picture?
a. The swine pictured the people of this world whom the devil controls to their own destruction.
9. Were both demon-possessed men saved by Jesus?
a. No, Jesus had compassion on one only, and the other man remained in his sin.
10. What did the people of that country do when they heard of that miracle?
a. These unbelieving and unthankful people pleaded with Jesus to leave their country.
Memory verse: “Be thou exalted, LORD, in thine own strength: so will we sing and praise thy power.”—Psalm 21:13
Matthew 8:28–34; Mark 5:1–20; Luke 8:22–39
Lesson 10: Jesus Casts Out Legion
1. What did Jesus tell the Pharisees who asked Jesus’ disciples why he ate with publicans and sinners?
a. Jesus said, “I am not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance.”
2. Who came to Jesus about that time as he was teaching his disciples?
a. Jairus, a ruler of the synagogue, came to Jesus and asked him to heal his only daughter who was sick.
3. Who came to Jesus seeking to be healed when Jesus was walking with the multitude to the house of Jairus?
a. A woman with an issue of blood for twelve years who had spent all her money on doctors who only made her sicker.
4. What happened to the woman when she touched the hem of Jesus’ garment?
a. Jesus felt power go out of him, and she was immediately healed.
5. Of what was that woman’s touch of Christ a sign?
a. Her touch was a sign of our faith, which is union with Christ.
6. What did messengers say to Jairus as the multitude approached his house?
a. The messengers said to Jairus, “Your daughter is dead.”
7. What did Jairus say to Jesus about his daughter?
a. Jairus said that if Jesus would only touch her she would live.
8. What did Jesus tell the people who surrounded the dead girl?
a. Jesus said that the girl was not dead but sleeping, and the people laughed him to scorn.
9. Why did Jesus say that the girl was asleep?
a. Jesus said she was asleep so that the people’s mockery could show that they knew the girl was really dead.
10. What did Jesus do?
a. Jesus took the girl by the hand and said, “Little girl, arise,” and she was raised from the dead.
Memory verse: “Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits: who forgiveth all thine iniquities; who healeth all thy diseases.”—Psalm 103:2–3
Lesson 11: Jesus’ Ministry in Galilee
1. What form of teaching did Jesus begin to use later in his ministry?
a. Jesus began to teach in parables.
2. What are parables?
a. Parables are earthly stories with spiritual meanings.
3. Why did Jesus teach in parables?
a. Jesus taught in parables to make the truth simple and to show that belief of the truth is not in man’s power but is God’s gift.
4. How did Jesus answer his disciples’ question about why he spoke to the multitudes in parables?
a. Jesus said he spoke in parables because to his disciples was given understanding of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven.
5. What is the first parable that Jesus spoke?
a. The first parable was of a man who sowed seed in his field.
6. What happened to the seed?
a. Some seed fell on good soil, while other seed fell on thorny and stoney soils and a hard path.
7. What is the good soil?
a. The good soil is God’s elect who hear his word, and the word bears fruit in them.
8. What is the thorny soil?
a. The thorny soil is the reprobate who appear to believe at first but then leave the truth because they love the world.
9. What is the stoney soil?
a. The stoney soil is also the reprobate who appear to believe, but when persecution comes they do not endure.
10. What is the hard path?
a. The hard path is the reprobate who hear the word, and the devil steals it away.
Memory verse: “Because it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.”—Matthew 13:11
Matthew 13:1–23; Mark 4:1–20; Luke 8:4–15
Lesson 12: Jesus Teaches in Parables and the Parable of the Sower
1. Why did Jesus rebuke the cities of Chorazin, Bethsaida, and Capernaum?
a. Jesus rebuked those cities because the people there had seen all Jesus’ mighty works and did not repent.
2. What did Jesus say about those wicked cities?
a. Jesus said that it would be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for those cities.
3. Where did Jesus go after that?
a. Jesus went to the land of Tyre and Sidon.
4. Who came to Jesus there?
a. A Canaanite woman came to Jesus.
5. What did that woman say to Jesus?
a. She said, “O Lord, thou Son of David, my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.”
6. How did Jesus answer the woman?
a. Jesus answered her not a word.
7. What did the disciples do when Jesus did not answer her?
a. The disciples told Jesus to send away the woman because she kept following them and shouting after them.
8. What did Jesus say then to the woman?
a. Jesus said, “I am sent to the lost sheep of the household of Israel, and it is not right to take the children’s bread and give it to dogs.”
9. How did the woman answer Jesus?
a. She said, “Truth, Lord, yet the dogs eat of the crumbs that fall from their masters’ table.”
10. How did Jesus respond to the woman?
a. Jesus said that the woman’s faith was great, and her daughter was healed that very hour.
Memory verse: “There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.”—Romans 15:12
Lesson 13: The Canaanite Woman
1. What did Jesus begin to show his disciples at this time?
a. Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and be killed by the Jews and be raised the third day.
2. What did Jesus say to Peter when he said, “Be it far from thee, Lord; this shall not be unto thee”?
a. Jesus said, “Get thee behind me, Satan.”
3. About what did Jesus warn his disciples at that time?
a. Jesus warned that whoever will come after Jesus must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow Jesus.
4. What happened about eight days later?
a. Jesus took Peter, James, and John into a mountain to pray, but the disciples fell asleep.
5. What did the disciples see in the mountain when they awoke?
a. The disciples saw Jesus’ face shine as the sun, his clothes shine white, and Jesus being transfigured.
6. What does it mean that Jesus was transfigured?
a. Jesus received a taste of the heavenly glory that he would receive after his resurrection.
7. Who appeared to Jesus on the mountain?
a. Moses and Elijah talked with Jesus about his death at Jerusalem.
8. What happened after Moses and Elijah appeared?
a. A bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from heaven said, “This is my beloved Son, hear him.”
9. What did the disciples ask after the cloud was gone?
a. The disciples asked why the scribes said that Jesus could not be the Christ because Elijah must come first.
10. What did Jesus answer the disciples?
a. Jesus said that Elijah had already come, and they had beheaded him, and Jesus would suffer at the hands of the scribes and Pharisees too.
Memory verse: “He received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”—2 Peter 1:17
Lesson 14: The Mount of Transfiguration
1. What did Jesus see as he came down from the mountain of transfiguration?
a. Jesus saw the disciples and the scribes arguing about Jesus and a great multitude gathered around them.
2. Why had that multitude gathered?
a. A man had asked the disciples to heal his lunatic son, but the disciples could not heal the boy.
3. What did the father say to Jesus?
a. The father said, “My son has an evil spirit of deafness and dumbness.”
4. What did that evil spirit do to the little boy?
a. The spirit tore at the boy, made him gnash his teeth and foam at the mouth, and often tried to kill him by casting him into the fire or water.
5. What did Jesus say to the father?
a. Jesus said, “By faith all things are possible.”
6. What did Jesus mean by that?
a. Jesus meant that faith must rest in the power of Christ alone.
7. What did the father answer?
a. He said, “Lord, I believe, help my unbelief.”
8. What did the evil spirit do to the boy when Jesus told the spirit to come out?
a. The spirit tore the boy, cried out, and left him as dead.
9. What did Jesus do to the little boy after the evil spirit did that to him?
a. Jesus as the one who has power over the devil took the boy by the hand and gave the boy to his father.
10. What did Jesus tell the disciples when they asked Jesus why they could not cast out the evil spirit?
a. Jesus said that the disciples could cast out the evil spirit because of their unbelief.
Memory verse: “The LORD shall increase you more and more, you and your children. Ye are blessed of the LORD which made heaven and earth.”—Psalm 115:14–15
Lesson 15: The Healing of the Lunatic Child
1. Why did Jesus go up to Jerusalem?
a. Jesus went to the feast of tabernacles.
2. What did Jesus cry on the last and great day of the feast?
a. Jesus cried, “If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.”
3. What did that mean?
a. Jesus meant that the people must believe on Jesus Christ for righteousness.
4. What did Jesus promise to the people?
a. Jesus promised that all who believe on him will have a river of living water flow out of their bellies.
5. What is the meaning of that river of living water?
a. The river of living water is the Spirit whom Jesus gives to all who are one with him by a true faith.
6. Where did Jesus go after the Jews in Jerusalem tried to kill him?
a. Jesus went across the Jordan River to Perea.
7. What did Jesus do in Perea?
a. Jesus taught the people in parables.
8. Name one of those parables?
a. Jesus taught the parable of the rich fool who laid up treasures for himself and was not rich toward God.
9. What did that fool in the parable do?
a. The fool had a plentiful harvest, so he tore down his old barns and made bigger barns and blessed himself that he would have an easy life for many years.
10. What did God say to the fool?
a. God said, “This night your soul will be required of thee.”
Memory verse: “Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the kingdom.”—Luke 12:32
Mark 10; Luke 12; John 7
Lesson 16: Jesus in Jerusalem and Perea
1. What news reached Jesus as he was in Perea?
a. Messengers said to Jesus, “Come quickly because Lazarus is sick.”
2. Who was Lazarus?
a. Lazarus was the brother of Mary and Martha, who were friends of Jesus.
3. Did Jesus go quickly to Lazarus?
a. No, Jesus waited four days.
4. Why did Jesus wait four days to go to Lazarus?
a. Jesus waited until Lazarus had died in order to show the power of God to raise Lazarus from the dead.
5. What did Martha say to Jesus when he arrived?
a. Martha said, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
6. What did Jesus answer Martha?
a. Jesus said, “I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believes in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.”
7. What did Jesus do when he saw where they had laid Lazarus?
a. Jesus wept.
8. What did Martha say to Jesus when he told the people by the grave to take away the stone?
a. Martha said, “Lord, by this time he stinks, for he has been dead four days.”
9. What did Jesus say at the opening of the grave?
a. Jesus said, “Lazarus, come forth.”
10. What did the Pharisees and the chief priests do when they heard that Jesus had raised Lazarus?
a. They immediately took counsel how they might destroy Jesus and Lazarus.
Memory verse: “Ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God. When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.”—Colossians 3:3–4
John 11; John 12:10–11
Lesson 17: The Raising of Lazarus
1. Where did Jesus go after the Jews sought to kill him?
a. Jesus went to Ephraim, for his hour was not yet come.
2. What did Jesus very plainly tell his disciples?
a. Jesus told his disciples that he was going to Jerusalem to suffer and die and rise the third day.
3. What miracle did Jesus perform as he traveled to Jerusalem?
a. Jesus healed blind Bartimaeus and his blind friend.
4. Whose home did Jesus enter in Jericho?
a. Jesus went to the home of Zacchaeus the publican.
5. What did Jesus say about Zacchaeus?
a. Jesus said that Zacchaeus was a true child of Abraham to whom Jesus brought salvation.
6. When did Jesus arrive near Jerusalem, and to whose home did he go?
a. About six days before the passover, Jesus came to the home of Mary, Martha, and Lazarus in Bethany.
7. What did Mary do to Jesus at a supper in the house of Simon the leper?
a. Mary anointed the feet of Jesus with precious ointment and wiped his feet with her hair.
8. What did that traitor Judas Iscariot say about what Mary had done to Jesus?
a. Judas said that Mary should have sold the precious ointment and given the money to the poor.
9. Did Judas really care about the poor?
a. No, Judas was a thief, and as the one who held the bag of money for the disciples, he wanted the money for himself.
10. What did Jesus say about what Mary had done?
a. Jesus said, “Leave her alone, for she has anointed me for my burial.”
Memory verse: “So Jesus had compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.”—Matthew 20:34
Matthew 20:30–34; Matthew 26:6–13; Mark 14:1–9; Luke 18–19; John 11:54–57; John 12:1–9
Lesson 18: Ephraim to Jerusalem
1. What did Jesus tell two of his disciples to do on Sunday?
a. Jesus told them to go into the next village to find a donkey and her colt tied and to bring them to him.
2. What did the disciples do after bringing the donkey and her colt to Jesus?
a. They piled their coats on the colt, and Jesus sat on it.
3. What did the multitude do when Jesus came riding on the colt toward
4. Jerusalem?
a. The people spread their garments and palm branches in front of Jesus and shouted, “Hosanna to the Son of David: Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.”
What did Jesus tell the Pharisees when they rebuked the people for praising
Jesus?
a. Jesus told the Pharisees that if the people were quiet the rocks would cry out.
5. Of what was Jesus’ royal entry into Jerusalem on a colt a sign?
a. Jesus royal entry on a colt was a sign that he will come into his kingdom through the lowliness of the cross.
6. What did Jesus do in the temple that day?
a. Jesus cast out the buyers and sellers.
7. What did Jesus say while he cast out the buyers and sellers?
a. Jesus said, “It is written, My house is the house of prayer, but you have made it a den of thieves.”
8. What is the significance of Jesus’ work of cleansing the temple?
a. The cleansing of the temple was a sign that Jesus Christ would cleanse God’s spiritual house from all sin by his cross.
9. What did Jesus say at that time about his death?
a. Jesus said, “If I be lifted up from the earth, I will draw all nations to myself.”
10. What did Jesus mean when he spoke of being lifted up?
a. Jesus meant his death on the cross where he was made a curse for us to deliver us from the curse of the law.
Memory verse: “Tell the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy King cometh unto thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.”—Matthew 21:5
Lesson 19: The Royal Entry into Jerusalem
1. What did Jesus prophesy about Jerusalem?
a. Jesus prophesied that Jerusalem and the temple would soon be destroyed.
2. Of what was that destruction of Jerusalem a sign?
a. Jerusalem’s destruction was a sign of the end of the world when Jesus will come again.
3. What was Jesus’ instruction to his disciples about his second coming and the end of the world?
a. Jesus said that there will be signs of his second coming.
4. What are some of those signs?
a. There will be wars and rumors of wars, earthquakes, famine, and pestilence.
5. Did Jesus speak of the most important sign?
a. Yes, Jesus said that the gospel of the kingdom must go to the ends of the earth, and then the end will come.
6. What did Jesus say about false prophets in the world before his second coming?
a. Jesus said that there will be many false prophets who do signs and wonders and deceive many.
7. How did Jesus describe the world before he comes again?
a. Jesus said that the people will be living as in the days of Noah and will not be looking for Jesus’ coming.
8. What will the world and false church do to Christ’s church?
a. They will hate the church and persecute God’s people.
9. How did Jesus comfort his church?
a. Jesus said that for the elect’s sake those days will be shortened.
10. Whom will Jesus send at his second coming?
a. Jesus will send his angels to gather his elect from the ends of the earth.
Memory verse: “Behold he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they also which pierced him: and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him. Even so, Amen.”— Revelation 1:7
Matthew 24; Mark 13; Luke 21
Lesson 20: Jesus’ Instruction About the End of the World
1. When Jesus instructed his disciples that he had to die, did they understand?
a. No, none understood except Judas Iscariot.
2. What did Judas do when he understood that Jesus had to die?
a. Judas was offended, and he decided to betray Christ.
3. To whom did Judas go?
a. Judas went to the chief priests and agreed to betray Jesus for thirty pieces of silver.
4. Why did Judas betray Jesus to the chief priests?
a. Judas betrayed Jesus because Judas was a reprobate appointed by God to this evil work.
5. Why did Jesus have to suffer that betrayal?
a. Jesus had to be betrayed because the sin of his people is a betrayal of their covenant relationship with God.
6. What did Jesus do with his disciples on Thursday night, the night he was betrayed?
a. Jesus and his disciples celebrated the last passover in the upper room.
7. What did Jesus institute after celebrating the last passover?
a. Jesus instituted the sacrament of the Lord’s supper and commanded his church to celebrate the Lord’s supper in remembrance of him.
8. Of what is the Lord’s supper a sign and seal?
a. The Lord’s supper is a sign and seal of the crucified body and shed blood of Christ that accomplished our salvation.
9. Why do we eat and drink the Lord’s supper?
a. We eat and drink the Lord’s supper as a sign that we eat and drink Jesus Christ by faith and are one body with him.
10. What did Jesus tell Judas after instituting the Lord’s supper?
a. Jesus said to Judas, “What thou doest, do quickly!”
Memory verse: “Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me.”—Psalm 41:9
Lesson 21: Betrayed and the Lord’s Supper
1. Where did Jesus lead his disciples after celebrating the last passover in the upper room?
a. Jesus took his disciples over the brook Kidron to the garden of Gethsemane.
2. What did Jesus do there?
a. Jesus took Peter, James, and John and went farther into the garden to pray.
3. What did Jesus pray?
a. Jesus prayed, “Father, if it be possible let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not my will but thy will be done.”
4. Who came to strengthen Jesus?
a. God sent an angel from heaven to comfort Jesus.
5. What happened to Jesus while he was praying?
a. Jesus sweat great drops of blood.
6. What did the disciples do while Jesus was praying?
a. All the disciples fell asleep.
7. Who came after Jesus was finished praying?
a. Judas Iscariot came with a large band of soldiers.
8. What did Judas do?
a. Judas betrayed Jesus with a kiss.
9. Could the soldiers have taken Jesus if he was not willing to go with them?
a. No, Jesus said that if he asked his Father, he would send twelve legions of angels.
10. How did Jesus show that he could not be taken against his will?
a. When Jesus spoke, all the soldiers fell down before him.
Memory verse: “It became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.”— Hebrews 2:10
Lesson 22: The Garden of Gethsemane
1. What did the disciples do when the soldiers bound Jesus.
a. The disciples were offended, and they all forsook Jesus and fled.
2. Where did the soldiers take Jesus first?
a. The soldiers took Jesus to the high priests Annas and Caiaphas.
3. How did the wicked Sanhedrin try to convict Jesus?
a. The Sanhedrin hired false witnesses against Jesus, but the false witnesses could not agree on a charge against him.
4. What did Peter do before the cock crew while Jesus was being tried?
a. With cursing and swearing, Peter denied Jesus three times as Jesus had said he would do.
5. What did Peter do when he heard the cock crow, and Jesus looked at Peter?
a. Peter remembered Jesus’ warning and Peter went out and wept bitterly.
6. Why did the Sanhedrin condemn Jesus?
a. The Sanhedrin condemned Jesus because he said that he was the Christ.
7. Where did the Jewish leaders take Jesus Friday morning?
a. The leaders took Jesus to Pontius Pilate, the Roman governor.
8. How did Pilate try to get rid of Jesus?
a. After hearing that Jesus was from Galilee, Pilate sent Jesus to King Herod.
9. What did the Jews do when Pilate gave them a choice between releasing Jesus or the murderer Barabbas?
a. The Jews said to release Barabbas and to crucify Jesus.
10. What did Pilate finally do?
a. Pilate released Barabbas and sent Jesus to be crucified, and then Pilate washed his hands.

Memory verse: “Of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together, for to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.”—Acts 4:27–28
Lesson 23: The Trial of Jesus
1. Where did the soldiers crucify Jesus?
a. Jesus was crucified outside Jerusalem at Golgotha, the place of the skull.
2. Whom did the soldiers compel to carry Jesus’ cross when he could no long carry it?
a. The soldiers made Simon the Cyrenian carry the cross.
3. What writing did Pilate hang on the cross over Jesus’ head?
a. Pilate hung the writing “JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.”
4. Why did Jesus have to die on a cross?
a. The death of the cross was accursed of God, and Jesus had to deliver us from God’s curse.
5. Who was crucified with Jesus?
a. The soldiers crucified Jesus between two thieves.
6. What did the soldiers do with Jesus’ clothes?
a. The soldiers divided his garments among themselves and cast lots for his coat.
7. What did Jesus pray as the soldiers were crucifying him?
a. Jesus prayed, “Father forgive them, for they know not what they do.”
8. Did Jesus pray that for every person there at the cross?
a. No, Jesus prayed only for his chosen people.
9. What did those who were standing by the cross do to Jesus?
a. The people mocked Jesus and said if he was the Christ he should come down from the cross.
10. What did Jesus tell the thief who said, “Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom?
a. Jesus said to the thief, “Today, thou shalt be with me in paradise.”
Memory verse: “Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: for it is written, Cursed is every one that hangeth on a tree.”—Galatians 3:13
Lesson 24: The Accursed Death of the Cross
1. For whom did Jesus lay down his life?
a. Jesus laid down his life for his sheep.
2. What did Jesus cry out during the three hours of darkness?
a. Jesus cried, “My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?”
3. What did Jesus say when he came out of the three hours of darkness?
a. Jesus said, “It is finished!”
4. What does “It is finished” mean?
a. “It is finished” means that Jesus fully accomplished all God’s will for the salvation of his elect people.
5. What happened in Jerusalem after Jesus died?
a. The veil of the temple was rent from top to bottom, and there was a great earthquake.
6. What did the rending of the veil mean?
a. The rending of the veil meant that Jesus had fulfilled the whole law and established perfect righteousness and that he alone is the way to the Father.
7. What did the centurion who was standing by say when he saw the earthquake?
a. The centurion said, “Truly this was the Son of God.”
8. What did the soldiers do to Jesus after they knew he was dead?
a. The soldiers pierced Jesus’ side with a spear, and out came blood and water.
9. How were the blood and water a sign of Jesus death?
a. The blood and water were a sign that Jesus’ death had cleansed his elect church from sin and made satisfaction to God.
10. Who went to Pilate to ask for the body of Jesus?
a. Nicodemus with Joseph of Arimathea asked for Jesus’ body, and they laid it in Joseph’s new tomb.
Memory verse: “I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.”— John 10:11
Lesson 25: It is Finished
1. What did the Jews ask of Pilate after Jesus was buried?
a. The Jews asked that the tomb be sealed and a guard placed there so that the disciples could not steal Jesus’ body.
2. What happened Sunday morning?
a. There was a great earthquake, and an angel descended from heaven to roll back the stone in front of Jesus’ tomb.
3. What happened to the guards?
a. The guards shook in fear and became as dead men.
4. What did the chief priests do after the guards reported what had happened at the tomb?
a. The chief priests gave money to the guards and told them to lie and say while they slept the disciples came and stole Jesus’ body.
5. Who met the women who were followers of Jesus when they got to the tomb?
a. Two angels met the women and said to them, “Fear not, Jesus is not here, for he is risen as he told you.”
6. What did the disciples think of the women’s report about what they had seen and heard at Jesus’ tomb?
a. The disciples thought that the women’s report was just an idle tale.
7. Who went to the tomb to see for themselves?
a. Peter and John ran, but John was faster and outran Peter.
8. What did Peter see when he entered the tomb?
a. Peter saw the linen clothes lying as they had been on Jesus and the linen face napkin wrapped by itself in a corner.
9. To whom did Jesus appear first?
a. Jesus first appeared to Mary Magdelene as she stood weeping by his tomb, but she thought Jesus was the gardener.
10. What did Jesus say to Mary?
a. Jesus said, “Mary,” and she immediately recognized Jesus.
Memory verse: “Who was delivered for our offences, and was raised again for our justification.”—Romans 4:25
Matthew 27:62–66; Matthew 28:1–15; Mark 16:1–9; Luke 24:1–12; John 20:1–18
Lesson 26: The Resurrection of Jesus
1. After Jesus’ resurrection why did he appear to his disciples?
a. Jesus appeared to the disciples so they could see him in his resurrected body and to teach them about his cross and resurrection.
2. To which disciples did Jesus appear as he traveled with them from Jerusalem to
3. Emmaus?
a. Jesus walked with Cleopas and another disciple, but they did not recognize Jesus.
4. What did Jesus teach Cleopas and the other disciple?
a. Jesus taught them from Moses and the prophets how Jesus had to suffer, die, and be raised from the dead.
5. What did Cleopas and the other disciple do when Jesus revealed himself to them at supper?
a. They immediately went back to Jerusalem to tell the disciples that Jesus was indeed risen.
6. When Jesus appeared to his disciples in the upper room and they thought they were seeing a spirit, what did Jesus do?
a. Jesus ate a little fish and some honeycomb to show the disciples that his body was real.
7. What disciple was missing when Jesus first appeared to his disciples in the upper room?
a. Thomas was not there.
8. Did Thomas believe when the other disciples told him they had seen Jesus?
a. No, Thomas said he would not believe unless he saw the nail prints in Jesus’ hands and put his fist in the spear hole in Jesus’ side.
When Jesus appeared the next week to all the disciples, what did Jesus tell
Thomas?
a. Jesus said to Thomas, “Put your finger in the nail hole and your fist in my side and be not faithless but believing.”
9. What did Thomas say to Jesus then?
a. Thomas said, “My Lord and my God!”
10. What did Jesus tell his disciples to do?
a. Jesus told them to preach repentance and remission of sins in his name throughout all nations.
Memory verse: “All things are of God, who hath reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation.”—2 Corinthians 5:18
Matthew 28:16–20; Mark 16:10–18; Luke 24:13–48; John 20:19–31; John 21; 1 Corinthians 15:1–20
Lesson 27: The Appearances of Jesus
New Testament History
The Coming of the Savior
Grades 1–4
1. What did Jesus do forty days after his resurrection?
a. Jesus gathered his disciples and told them not to leave Jerusalem until they had received the Holy Ghost.
2. What did Jesus say would happen after the disciples had received the Holy
Ghost?
a. The disciples would be witnesses of Jesus Christ in Jerusalem, Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth.
3. What happened when the disciples were gathered on the Mount of Olives?
a. Jesus was taken up from the earth, and a cloud received him out of their sight.
4. What was that event?
a. That event was the ascension of Jesus, in which he went from earth to heaven.
5. What happened to Jesus in the ascension?
a. God sat Jesus Christ at God’s right hand.
6. What does it mean that Christ sits at God’s right hand?
a. God gave to Christ highest glory and all authority in heaven and on earth.
7. What did two angels tell the disciples as they stood looking up into heaven?
a. The angels said that Jesus will come again as the disciples had seen him go.
8. What did the disciples do when they came back to Jerusalem after the ascension on the Mount of Olives?
a. The disciples chose a man to replace Judas Iscariot.
9. Why did the disciples replace Judas Iscariot with another man?
a. The disciples understood what David had written in the psalms that the traitor’s office had to be given to another man.
10. How did the disciples choose another man?
a. The disciples chose the man by lot because the Spirit had not yet been poured out.
11. Whom did the disciples choose?
a. The disciples chose Matthias, who was numbered as an apostle with the others.
Memory verse: “And hath put all things under his feet, and gave him to be head over all things to the church.”—Ephesians 1:22
Acts 1
Lesson 1: The Ascension of Jesus Christ
1. What happened fifty days after the resurrection of Jesus Christ?
a. At Pentecost Christ poured out his Spirit on his church in Jerusalem.
2. Of what was Pentecost and the pouring out of the Spirit the fulfillment?
a. Pentecost was the fulfillment of the Old Testament feast of Pentecost.
3. What three signs accompanied the outpouring of the Spirit?
a. The signs were the sound as of a mighty rushing wind, cloven tongues of fire, and the 120 believers spoke in many languages.
4. What did some of the people who were gathered at Pentecost say about the disciples when they spoke in different languages?
a. Some said that the disciples were drunk.
5. How did Peter respond to that accusation?
a. Peter preached a sermon to the people.
6. What did Peter say to the people?
a. Peter said that with wicked hands the Jews had crucified Jesus according to God’s sovereign counsel.
7. What did Peter say that God did to Jesus, whom the Jews had crucified?
a. Peter said that God had raised Jesus from the dead and gave to him the promise of the Spirit.
8. What did Peter tell those in the multitude who were pricked in their hearts?
a. Peter said, “Repent and be baptized in the name of Jesus for the remission of sins.”
9. What did Peter’s command mean?
a. The command meant that God had forgiven all the sins of his people through Christ and that God pricked their hearts and gave baptism as the assurance of their righteousness in Christ.
10. How did Peter explain God’s gracious forgiveness of sins?
a. Peter said, “The promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are afar off, even as many as the Lord our God shall call.”
11. What is the promise that Peter preached?
a. The promise is God’s oath to all his elect and their spiritual seed that he freely forgives their sins by faith alone.

Memory verse: “It shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions: and also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my spirit.”—Joel 2:28–29
Acts 2
Lesson 2: Pentecost
1. Who met Peter and John as they went into the temple?
a. Peter and John were met by a beggar who had been lame from birth.
2. When the lame beggar asked Peter for money, what did Peter say?
a. Peter said, “Silver and gold have I none, but in the name of Jesus Christ rise up and walk.”
3. What happened to the lame beggar when Peter said that to him?
a. The beggar’s legs received strength, and he went with Peter and John into the temple leaping and praising God.
4. Of what was that miracle an example for the church?
a. That miracle was an example of what the living and exalted Jesus Christ continues to do through his church.
5. What did Peter preach to the people who were gathered around and wondering what had happened to the lame beggar?
a. Peter preached that they had killed the Prince of life, but God raised him; and by faith in the name of Jesus Christ, the man was healed.
6. How did Peter explain that Jesus Christ had performed the miracle?
a. Peter explained that God had sent Jesus to bless the people by turning them away from their iniquities.
7. What did the priests and Sadducees who were angry about the Peter’s preaching the resurrection of Jesus do to Peter and John?
a. The priests and Sadducees laid hold on Peter and John and put them in prison.
8. The next day when the priests and Sadducees asked Peter and John by whose power they performed the miracles, what did Peter say?
a. Peter said that they performed miracles by the name Jesus Christ, who is the stone rejected by the builders but who is become the head of the corner.
9. What did the priests and Sadducees tell Peter and John the next morning?
a. The priests and Sadducees threatened Peter and John and commanded them not to teach anymore in the name of Jesus.
10. How did Peter respond?
a. Peter said, “We cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard.”
Memory verse: “Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word.”—Acts 4:29
Acts 3–4:31
Lesson 3: The Healing of the Lame Man
1. What do we read of the church after Pentecost?
a. God added daily to the church such as should be saved.
2. What was the spiritual condition of the church after Pentecost?
a. The members were all united by the Spirit in the truth, and no one lacked anything because the members shared what they had.
3. Who especially contributed to the relief of the poor in the church by selling his land?
a. Barnabas, a Levite from Cyprus, sold his land and laid the money at the feet of the apostles.
4. What did Ananias and Sapphira do when they sold a possession and brought the money to the apostles?
a. Ananias and Sapphira secretly kept back part of the money for themselves.
5. What did Peter tell Ananias that he had done?
a. Peter told Ananias that he had lied to the Holy Ghost.
6. What happened to Ananias?
a. Ananias fell down dead, and the young men carried him out and buried him.
7. Who came to Peter about three hours later?
a. Sapphira came to Peter, and she also lied to the Holy Ghost.
8. What did Peter tell her?
a. Peter said that she had agreed with her husband to tempt the Spirit of the Lord.
9. Did Sapphira die also?
a. Yes, Sapphira fell down dead, and the young men buried her by her husband.
10. Of what are Ananias and Sapphira a warning?
a. They are a warning that God will judge hypocrites in the church who make a false confession.
Memory Work: “The natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.”—1 Corinthians 2:14
Lesson 4: Ananias and Sapphira
1. What did the apostles do after the ascension of Jesus?
a. The apostles worked many signs and wonders, healed many sick, and cast out unclean spirits.
2. Who persecuted the apostles because of their miracles?
a. The high priest and the Sadducees put the apostles in prison.
3. What did the angel of the Lord do that night because the apostles were in prison?
a. The angel of the Lord opened the prison and told the apostles to go and preach in the temple.
4. What did the officers find when they went to take the apostles out of prison?
a. The officers found the prison door shut and the guards in front of the doors, but the apostles were not in the prison.
5. How did the high priest and Sadducees find out where the apostles were?
a. A man came and told them that the apostles were teaching the people in the temple.
6. What did the high priest say to the apostles when the Sanhedrin took them?
a. The high priest said, “We told you not to teach in the name of Jesus, but you fill Jerusalem with your doctrine and bring his blood upon us.”
7. How did Peter respond to the high priest?
a. Peter said, “We will obey God rather than men.”
8. What was Peter’s explanation of Jesus’ crucifixion to the Sanhedrin?
a. Peter accused the Sanhedrin of crucifying Jesus, but God raised Jesus and exalted him to God’s right hand to give repentance and remission of sins to Israel.
9. What did the Sanhedrin then plan to do?
a. The Sanhedrin planned to put the apostles to death.
10. What wicked advice did the Pharisee Gamaliel give to the Sanhedrin about the apostles?
a. Gamaliel advised, “Leave them alone: If their cause is of men, it will come to nothing; but if their cause is of God, you cannot fight against it.”
Memory verse: “Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.”—Matthew 5:11
Lesson 5: The Apostles Imprisoned
1. What trouble arose when the church grew and had many members?
a. In caring for the poor, the church did not care for the Grecian widows.
2. What did the apostles tell the church?
a. The apostles told the church to choose seven deacons to care for the needy.
3. Who was one of the deacons whom the church chose?
a. The church chose Stephen, a man full of the Holy Spirit and faith, who did wonders and miracles among the people.
4. What did the enemies of the church do about Stephen?
a. The enemies hired false witnesses, who lied that Stephen spoke blasphemous words against the law of Moses and God.
5. What did Stephen preach first to the Pharisees and Sadducees of the Sanhedrin?
a. Stephen rehearsed the history of God’s faithfulness to his covenant from Abraham to Moses.
6. What did Stephen say that the Jews had done with Moses?
a. Stephen said that the Jews had rejected Moses and turned their hearts back to Egypt and worshiped idols.
7. Of what did Stephen accuse the Pharisees and Sadducees?
a. Stephen accused them of being stiff-necked and always resisting the Holy Ghost as did their fathers.
8. What did Stephen accuse the Pharisees and Sadducees of doing to Jesus?
a. Stephen accused them of being murderers of Jesus, just as their fathers had murdered the prophets who spoke of Christ.
9. What did Stephen preach about Israel and the law?
a. Stephen preached that Israel had received the law by angels, but Israel never kept the law.
10. What did the Pharisees and Sadducees do to Stephen?
a. In their hatred they rushed on Stephen and bit him with their teeth.
11. What did Stephen say to those who captured him?
a. Stephen said, “I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.”
12. What did the Pharisees and Sadducees do to Stephen after that?
a. They cast Stephen out of the city and stoned him.
Memory verse: “We have sinned with our fathers, we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.”—Psalm 106:6
Acts 6–7
Lesson 6: Deacons and Stephen
1. Who held the coats of those who stoned Stephen?
a. Saul of Tarsus held their coats.
2. Who was this Saul from Tarsus?
a. Saul was a Jew who was raised as a Pharisee by the teacher Gamaliel in Jerusalem.
3. What did Saul do after the death of Stephen?
a. Saul led a terrible persecution of the church.
4. What did Saul say that he did in that persecution?
a. Saul entered every house, dragged men and women to prison, made them blaspheme, and even put some of them to death.
5. What was the effect of Saul’s persecution?
a. Saul made havoc in the church, and the church was scattered.
6. Was the church hurt by that persecution led by Saul?
a. No, all those who were scattered preached the gospel, and the word was spread throughout the land.
7. What happened to Saul as he traveled to Damascus to persecute the church there?
a. A bright line shone and blinded Saul, and Jesus appeared and said, “Saul, Saul why do you persecute me?”
8. What did Saul’s companions do?
a. They led Saul blind into the city of Damascus.
9. Whom did God send to help Saul?
a. God sent Ananias, who gave Saul his sight back and baptized him.
10. Where did Saul go after his conversion?
a. Saul went to Arabia for three years to be taught the gospel by Jesus.
11. What did Saul do in Damascus immediately after leaving Arabia?
a. Saul taught that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God.
12. How did Saul escape a plot of the Jews to kill him?
a. The disciples let Saul down over the city wall in a basket.
Memory verse: “He is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel.”—Acts 9:15
Acts 7:58–60; Acts 8:1–4; Acts 9:1–31; Acts 21:37–22:21; Acts 26:4–21; 2 Corinthians 11:31-32; Galatians 1:11–2:16; Philippians 3:4–6
Lesson 7: Persecution by Saul of Tarsus and His Conversion
1. Which member of the church was scattered during Saul’s persecution?
a. The deacon Philip, who went to Samaria and preached the gospel to the people, and many believed it with joy and were baptized.
2. What did God do through Philip as signs of his preaching?
a. Philip cast out many unclean spirits and healed the sick and lame.
3. Who was converted at that time?
a. Simon the Sorcerer was converted, who for a long time had bewitched the people with his sorceries.
4. What did Peter and John do when they heard that the Samaritans had received the word?
a. Peter and John came and laid their hands on the people, and they received the Holy Ghost.
5. What did Simon do when he saw that the apostles gave the Spirit through the laying on of hands?
a. Simon offered the apostles money for that power.
6. What did Peter say to Simon?
a. Peter said, “Your money perish with you because you think the gift of God can be bought.”
7. Where did the Spirit send Philip after Samaria?
a. The Spirit sent Philip to the Ethiopian eunuch, who was returning from Jerusalem.
8. Where did Philip find the eunuch?
a. The eunuch was sitting in his chariot reading in the prophet Isaiah.
9. What did the eunuch ask Philip?
a. The eunuch asked who Isaiah meant by the man who was taken as a lamb to the slaughter.
10. Whom did Philip preach to the eunuch?
a. Philip preached Jesus Christ and baptized the eunuch.
11. What happened to Philip after he baptized the eunuch?
a. The Spirit of God caught Philip up, and the eunuch saw him no more.

Memory verse: “He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth: he is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, and as a sheep before her shearers is dumb, so he openeth not his mouth.”—Isaiah 53:7
Acts 8
Lesson 8: The Gospel Spreads to Samaria
1. Who was Cornelius?
a. Cornelius was a God-fearing Roman centurion who lived in Caesarea.
2. What did the angel of God tell Cornelius in a vision about the ninth hour one day?
a. The angel of God told Cornelius to send messengers to Joppa to get Peter.
3. What vision did Peter see the next day at Simon the tanner’s house about the sixth hour when he was hungry for lunch?
a. Peter saw heaven opened and a large sheet tied by the four corners full of clean and unclean animals.
4. What did a voice from heaven tell Peter?
a. The voice said, “Rise, Peter; kill and eat.”
5. What did Peter respond?
a. Peter said, “Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten anything common or unclean.”
6. What did the Lord tell Peter?
a. The Lord said, “What God has cleansed, do not call common.”
7. How many times did that happen?
a. Three times the sheet came down, and then the sheet was taken up to heaven.
8. What happened as Peter was wondering what that vision meant?
a. Three messengers came to the house looking for Peter, and the Spirit told Peter to go with them.
9. When Peter came to Cornelius’ house, what did Peter preach to Cornelius and his household?
a. Peter preached that through the name of Christ whoever believes on him receives the remission of sins.
10. How did God witness that Cornelius and his household were his elect whose sins he had forgiven?
a. God gave the Holy Ghost to them, and they spoke in tongues.
11. How did Peter respond to the accusations of certain members of the church who accused him of eating with the uncircumcised?
a. Peter said that the Holy Ghost fell on Cornelius and his household as proof that God granted them faith and repentance.
Memory verse: “When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.”—Acts 11:18
Lesson 9: The Vision of the Great Sheet: The Gospel to the Gentiles
1. Who began a persecution of the church at this time?
a. King Herod began a persecution and killed James the brother of John with the sword.
2. Whom else did Herod persecute when he saw that killing James pleased the
Jews?
a. Herod put Peter in prison and intended to kill him after Easter as a show
1. for the Jews.
4. What happened to Peter while he was sleeping and chained to two soldiers in the prison the night before he was to die?
a. The angel of the Lord appeared and hit Peter on his side, and his chains fell off.
5. What did the angel say to Peter?
a. The angel said, “Get dressed and follow me,” but Peter thought he was seeing a vision.
6. What did the angel do with Peter?
a. The angel led Peter past all the guards and out the iron gate of the prison and then disappeared.
7. Whose house did Peter go to after he was out of the prison?
a. Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John Mark, where the church had been praying for Peter.
8. What happened when the little girl Rhoda saw Peter knocking at the door of the house, and she ran and told the people that Peter was outside?
a. The people thought that she was crazy, but Peter kept knocking until he was let inside the house.
9. What did Peter say to the people when he was let inside the house?
a. He told them that the angel of the Lord had delivered him, and then Peter left.
What did Herod do to the soldiers when he discovered that Peter was not in prison?
a. Herod commanded that all the soldiers who had been guarding Peter be killed.
10. What did the people of Tyre and Sidon say about Herod one day as he was speaking to them?
a. They said, “It is the voice of god and not of a man!”
11. What did the angel of the Lord do to Herod?
a. The angel smote Herod with worms and he died because he did not give glory to God.
Memory verse: “The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? the LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?”—Psalm 27:1
Acts 12
Lesson 10: Herod’s Persecution of the Church
1. What did the Holy Ghost say to the company of prophets and teachers who labored in Antioch?
a. The Holy Ghost said, “Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
2. What method of preaching did Paul use in all his missionary labors?
a. Paul preached first to the Jews and then to the Gentiles.
3. Where did the Holy Spirit first lead Paul and Barnabas?
a. The Holy Spirit led them to sail from Antioch to the island of Cyprus.
4. What did Paul and Barnabas do on the island of Cyprus?
a. They preached the word of God in all the Jewish synagogues until they came to Paphos.
5. What sorcerer opposed Paul when he preached the word of God to Sergius Paulus, the Roman governor?
a. Paul was opposed by Barjesus, called Elymas, a Jewish false prophet who tried to turn the governor from the faith.
6. What did Paul say by the Holy Ghost to Elymas?
a. Paul said that Elymas was a son of the devil, an enemy of all righteousness who twisted God’s ways, and that he would be blind for a season.
7. What do we read of the governor Sergius Paulus when he saw these things?
a. He believed, being astonished at the Lord’s doctrine.
8. What is the Lord’s doctrine?
a. The Lord’s doctrine is that salvation is of God alone and is not in man’s power.
9. Whom had Paul and Barnabas taken with them who later turned back when they came into Pamphylia?
a. John Mark turned back and went to Jerusalem.
10. What did Paul tell the Jews in Antioch concerning the cross and resurrection of Christ?
a. Paul said that all who believe in Christ are justified from all the things from which they could not be justified by the law of Moses.
11. What did Paul tell the Jews of Antioch in Pisidia when they rejected the word concerning Jesus Christ?
a. Paul said that it was necessary for the word to be preached first to the Jews, but now he would go to the Gentiles.
12. How does the Holy Spirit explain that many Jews rejected the word, but many Gentiles believed?
a. The Holy Spirit says, “As many as were ordained to eternal life believed.”
Memory verse: “He saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.”—Romans 9:15
Acts 12:25-Acts 13
Lesson 11: The First Missionary Journey (1)
1. Where did Paul and Barnabas go on their first missionary journey after Antioch in Pisidia?
a. Paul and Barnabas went to Iconium, where many Jews and Gentiles believed.
2. How did God give testimony of his word of grace?
a. God granted signs and wonders to be done by Paul and Barnabas.
3. How did the unbelieving Jews oppose the ministry of Paul and Barnabas?
a. The unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles against those who believed.
4. What was the result of the Jews’ evil work?
a. The city was divided, and part followed the apostles and part followed the Jews.
5. How was Paul forced to flee Iconium?
a. The unbelieving Gentiles and the Jews tried to capture Paul and Barnabas to beat and stone them.
6. What notable miracle did Paul perform in Lystra?
a. Paul healed a man who had been a cripple from birth.
7. What did the Gentiles in Lystra do to Paul and Barnabas because they thought Paul and Barnabas were the pagan gods Jupiter and Mercury?
a. The Gentiles tried to sacrifice to Paul and Barnabas.
8. How did the wicked Jews from Antioch and Iconium oppose Paul in Lystra?
a. The Jews persuaded the people to stone Paul and leave him for dead.
9. What did God do for Paul?
a. As the disciples stood around, God raised up Paul, and he walked back into the city.
10. What did Paul and Barnabas do when they returned to Antioch at the end of the first missionary journey?
a. Paul and Barnabas returned to all the churches that they had established and ordained elders in every city.
Memory verse: “Now in Christ Jesus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of Christ.”—Ephesians 2:13
Acts 14; 2 Corinthians 12:1–5
Lesson 12: The First Missionary Journey (2)
1. What had Jesus Christ made clear through the conversion of Cornelius and his household and by the first missionary journey?
a. Jesus Christ made clear that he would gather his church from the Gentiles.
2. Who caused trouble in the church about the Gentiles?
a. False teachers from Judea taught the Gentiles that they had to be circumcised in order to be saved.
3. Why was that teaching false doctrine?
a. The doctrine was false because it taught that there is that which man must do to be saved.
4. What did the churches do in response to that controversy?
a. The elders of the churches gathered in Jerusalem to settle the controversy according to the word of God.
5. What did Peter say about the Gentiles?
a. Peter said that God testified by giving the Holy Ghost to Cornelius and his household that God had purified their hearts by faith.
6. What else did Peter say?
a. Peter said that the apostles believed that Jews and Gentiles are saved by grace alone.
7. What did Paul and Barnabas explain to the people?
a. They explained that by the many miracles and wonders worked among the Gentiles, God testified that he saved Gentiles by grace alone.
8. What did James the brother of Jesus say about the gathering of the Gentiles?
a. James said that their gathering was the fulfillment of God’s promise to build the broken-down house of David.
9. What did the church decide by the Holy Spirit about the necessity of the Gentiles to keep the law of Moses?
a. The church decided that there is no commandment that the Gentiles had to keep the law of Moses.
10. Why did the apostles tell the Gentiles not to eat meat offered to idols or from strangled things?
a. The apostles said that to remind the Gentiles not to offend weak brethren in the church.
11. Why did the apostles tell the Gentiles not to commit fornication?
a. The apostles said that to remind the Gentiles not to use their liberty in Christ as an occasion to sin.
Memory verse: “Brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.”—Galatians 5:13
Acts 15
Lesson 13: Controversy Regarding the Salvation of the Gentiles
1. What happened at the beginning of the second missionary journey?
a. Paul would not take John Mark, but Barnabas wanted to take him.
2. What was the result of that disagreement?
a. Barnabas and John Mark went to Cyprus, and Paul took Silas.
3. Whom did Paul take into the ministry when he came again to the churches in
Galatia?
a. Paul took Timothy, whose mother Eunice and grandmother Lois were Jews and believers, but his father was a Greek.
4. What did Paul require regarding Timothy?
a. Paul had Timothy circumcised for the sake of his ministry to the Jews.
5. Where did the Holy Ghost forbid Paul to preach?
a. The Holy Ghost forbade Paul to preach the word in Asia and Bithynia.
6. What vision did Paul receive when he was in Troas.
a. Paul saw a Macedonian man who said, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”
7. What did that vision mean?
a. The vision meant that God had called Paul to preach the gospel in Macedonia.
8. Where did Paul go in Macedonia?
a. Paul went to Philippi, a Roman colony and the chief city of Macedonia.
9. Where did Paul preach first?
a. Paul preached the word to the women who worshiped by the river.
10. Who believed Paul’s preaching?
a. Lydia, a woman of Thyatira and a seller of purple, believed because the Lord had opened her heart.
Memory verse: “Being born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and abideth for ever.”—1 Peter 1:23
Lesson 14: The Second Missionary Journey (1)
1. Who met Paul as he and Silas preached day by day?
a. They were met by a little slave girl who had an evil spirit and whose masters made money from her fortune-telling.
2. What did the girl say about Paul and Silas?
a. She said, “These men are servants of the most high God who show the way of salvation.”
3. Why was Paul grieved with the devil in that little girl?
a. Paul was grieved because there is no fellowship between Christ and devils.
4. What did the girl’s masters do when Paul cast out the devil from her?
a. They caught Paul and Silas and took them to the magistrates, who beat them and put them in prison.
5. What did the Lord send that night while Paul and Silas were in prison?
a. The Lord sent a great earthquake that opened all the prison doors and loosened all the chains on the prisoners.
6. What did Paul tell the keeper of the prison when he tried to kill himself?
a. Paul said, “Do not harm yourself, for we are all here!”
7. What did the Philippian jailer ask Paul and Silas?
a. The jailer said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”
8. Why did the jailer ask that question?
a. He asked because God had caused a spiritual earthquake in the jailer’s heart, and he understood his sin and guilt.
9. What did Paul tell the jailer?
a. Paul said, “Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved and thy house.”
10. What did Paul mean by that?
a. Paul meant that the jailer must do nothing for his salvation, but that he and his house were saved by faith only.
11. Why did Paul speak of the jailer’s house?
a. Paul said that because God’s unconditional covenant promise is to believers and their elect seed.
Memory verse: “By grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God.”—Ephesians 2:8
Lesson 15: Second Missionary Journey (2)
1. In the morning when the magistrates of Philippi tried to send Paul and Silas away secretly, what did they tell the magistrates?
a. Paul and Silas said that they were Romans who had been beaten uncondemned and cast into prison.
2. How did the magistrates react when they heard that?
a. They were afraid, so they asked Paul and Silas to leave the city.
3. What did the Jews in Thessalonica do when they saw that many Greeks and chief women believed the gospel?
a. The Jews gathered a mob and attacked the house of Jason where Paul was staying.
4. Where did the church of Thessalonica send Paul to keep him safe?
a. The church sent Paul to Berea.
5. What did Paul find concerning the Berean Jews?
a. The Bereans searched the scriptures daily about what Paul preached to see whether it was true.
6. When the Bereans compared Paul’s teaching and scripture, what did they find?
a. They found that Paul spoke the truth, and many Bereans believed.
7. Where did Paul go when he was driven out of Berea by the wicked Jews of
8. Thessalonica?
a. Paul came to Athens and found the city completely given over to idolatry.
What did Paul preach to the Athenians when he saw an altar to the unknown
God?
a. Paul said that the unknown God is the true God who raised Christ and appointed him to be judge at the final resurrection.
9. How did the Jews of Corinth oppose Paul’s preaching of Jesus Christ?
a. The Jews made accusations against Paul before Gallio, the governor.
10. What did Gallio do when he heard the Jews’ accusation against Paul?
a. Gallio drove the Jews from his judgment seat and did not care when the Greeks beat Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue.
11. What comforting vision did God give to Paul in Corinth?
a. The Lord comforted Paul with the truth of God’s election: “Be not afraid but speak, for I have much people in this city.”
Memory verse: “I endure all things for the elect’s sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.”—2 Timothy 2:10
Lesson 16: The Second Missionary Journey (3)
1. How did Paul begin his third missionary journey?
a. Paul visited all the churches of Galatia that he had established on his second missionary journey and came at last to Ephesus.
2. Whom did Paul meet in Ephesus?
a. Paul met about twelve disciples who had been baptized by John the Baptist in the name Jesus but had not received the Holy Ghost.
3. What happened when Paul laid his hands on those disciples?
a. They received the Holy Ghost and spoke in tongues and prophesied.
4. What did that coming of the Holy Ghost confirm about John the Baptist?
a. That confirmed the ministry of John the Baptist, who said that Jesus would forgive sins and baptize with the Holy Ghost.
5. What do we read of Paul’s ministry in Ephesus?
a. Paul preached many months until the people in Ephesus and the surrounding cities heard the gospel of Jesus Christ.
6. How did God confirm the truth of the gospel preached by Paul?
a. God worked special miracles by Paul, so that even a handkerchief or an apron from Paul healed the sick and drove out evil spirits.
7. What evil magicians tried to imitate the power of God in Paul?
a. Sceva and his seven sons, who were vagabond Jews, priests, and exorcists, tried to imitate Paul’s power.
8. What did the evil spirit whom they tried to cast out say to those men?
a. The evil spirit said, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?”
9. What did the man with the evil spirit do to Sceva and his sons?
a. The man attacked and beat Sceva and his sons and they fled naked and wounded.
10. What did many in Ephesus do when they heard of the beating of Sceva and his sons?
a. They took all their books of sorcery, worth about fifty thousand pieces of silver, and burned them.

Memory verse: “John answered…, I indeed baptize you with water; but one mightier than I cometh, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to unloose: he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost and with fire.”—Luke 3:16
Lesson 17: The Third Missionary Journey (1)
1. What the result of Paul’s preaching at Ephesus and in the surrounding area?
a. Many turned from the worship of the idol Diana and served the Lord.
2. What happened in the latter part of Paul’s ministry in Ephesus?
a. Demetrius the silversmith persuaded the other craftsmen to raise a riot against Paul.
3. Why were Demetrius and the other craftsmen angry?
a. They were angry because they made silver shrines for the temple of the goddess Diana in Ephesus and many stopped buying them.
4. What did the craftsmen say had happened since Paul came?
a. The craftsmen said that Paul had destroyed their businesses and caused the temple of the goddess Diana to be despised.
5. What did the craftsmen shout?
a. They shouted, “Great is Diana of the Ephesians!”
6. What happened when their shouting created a riot in the whole city?
a. The people dragged Paul’s companions Gaius and Aristarchus into the amphitheater.
7. What did Paul want to do?
a. Paul wanted to address the mob, but his friends persuaded him not to go to the amphitheater.
8. What was going on in the amphitheater?
a. Most of the people in the amphitheater did not know why they were there because the assembly was confused.
9. What did the mob do when Alexander the Jew tried to speak?
a. When the people found out that Alexander was a Jew, they shouted for two hours, “Great is Diana of the Ephesians.”
10. Who finally quieted the riot?
a. The town clerk told Demetrius and the craftsmen that they could take their case to a lawful assembly, and the clerk dismissed the people.
11. What did Paul do after the uproar had been stilled?
a. Paul left for Macedonia, where he preached in the churches, and then went to Corinth.

Memory verse: “What agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.”—2 Corinthians 6:16
Lesson 18: Third Missionary Journey (2)
1. What changed Paul’s plans to sail from Corinth to Jerusalem?
a. Paul discovered a plot of the Jews to kill him.
2. What miracle did Paul perform on the young man Eutychus in Troas?
a. Paul raised Eutychus, who had fallen asleep while Paul was preaching and fell out of a third story window and died.
3. Whom did Paul meet in Miletus?
a. Paul met the elders from the church in Ephesus.
4. About what did Paul warn those elders concerning the church in Ephesus?
a. Paul warned that after he departed false teachers would arise.
5. How did Paul describe these false teachers?
a. He called them grievous wolves who would not spare the flock.
6. What did Paul command the elders to do in light of that danger?
a. The elders had to take heed to the flock that God had purchased with his own blood.
7. Where did Paul stay in Caesarea?
a. Paul stayed in the house of Philip the evangelist, who had four daughters who prophesied.
8. Who came to Paul at Philip’s house?
a. The prophet Agabus came and bound Paul with his own girdle.
9. What did Agabus prophesy about Paul?
a. Agabus said, “The Jews will bind the man who owns this girdle and deliver him to the Gentiles.”
10. What did Paul’s companions do?
a. They pleaded with Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
11. What did Paul say to his companions?
a. Paul said, “Why do you weep and break my heart? I am ready to be bound and to die in Jerusalem for Christ’s sake.”
Memory verse: “I think that God hath set forth us the apostles last, as it were appointed to death: for we are made a spectacle unto the world, and to angels, and to men.”—1 Corinthians 4:9
Lesson 19: The Third Mission Journey (3)
1. When Paul came to James the brother of Jesus and the elders in Jerusalem, what did they tell Paul about his enemies?
a. James and the elders told Paul that enemies were slandering his doctrine by saying that he taught the Jews to hate the law of Moses.
2. What did the elders in Jerusalem suggest that Paul should do?
a. They said that Paul should join four men of the church in a Nazarite vow.
3. Why did Paul join those four men in a vow?
a. Paul showed that Jesus Christ is the end of the law for righteousness and that it was no sin to live like a Jew.
4. What happened while Paul was in the temple?
a. The enemies of the gospel from Asia said that Paul defiled the temple by taking Greeks into it.
5. What did those enemies do to Paul?
a. They dragged Paul out of the temple and were going to kill him.
6. Who rescued Paul?
a. Claudius Lysius, the Roman chief captain, came with his soldiers to rescue Paul.
7. What did that incident show?
